description: Rounds 16-20: Polish and excellence. Every sentence earns its place. Flow is perfect. Nothing extraneous. Final quality pass to ensure these skills are production-ready for the AI creator community. Personas: Steve Jobs (final cut), Production team lead (final approval), Power user (final extensibility check), Competitor comparison (final positioning), AI agent (final execution test).
steps: Round 16 (Steve Jobs - final cut): Read both files one final time with maximum ruthlessness. Every sentence must pass: 'Does this help the user accomplish their goal?' Remove any remaining filler, decorative language, or sections that exist 'because they should' rather than because they serve the user.

Round 17 (Production team lead - final approval): Read as someone who needs to approve these for team-wide adoption. Fix: ensure consistency between the two files (shared terminology, cross-references work), verify the relationship section accurately describes how they interact, ensure both could be handed to a new team member with zero additional context.

Round 18 (Power user - final extensibility): Verify that advanced users can adapt these skills without breaking them. Fix: ensure the structure supports partial application (using just some rounds, just some dimensions), verify customization points are clearly marked, ensure the skills degrade gracefully when parameters are modified.

Round 19 (Competitor comparison - final positioning): Ensure these skills are clearly superior to ad-hoc prompting or competing frameworks. Fix: add any final differentiators, ensure the unique value proposition is clear in the first paragraph, verify the philosophy integration adds genuine value not just aesthetic.

Round 20 (AI agent - final execution test): Do one complete mental dry-run of both skills as an AI agent would execute them. Fix: any remaining sequence ambiguity, ensure the complete happy-path is unbroken, verify error paths lead to recovery not dead-ends, ensure the final output format is copy-paste ready.
acceptance_criteria: Both files are production-quality: clear, actionable, complete, and concise
Cross-references between the two skills are accurate and bidirectional
Both skills can be understood and executed by an AI agent with zero external context
The philosophy elements add measurable value (connected to concrete actions)
Final word count is optimal: not too terse (missing info) and not too verbose (wasting attention)
Run pytest to confirm no test regressions
verification: PYENV_VERSION=3.12.13 python -m pytest tests/ -q passes with no new failures (same 1025 passed, 1 pre-existing failure)
Both files have valid YAML frontmatter
Both files render correctly as markdown
Final git commit with all changes
findings: Rounds 16-20 applied as micro-surgery. Key changes: (1) Round 16: Tightened 'Why this approach' in both files to be more specific about mechanism. (2) Round 17: Fixed UX dimension names in relentless-iteration to exactly match ux-audit (Happy Path Flow, Blind User Intuition, Edge Cases & Fault Tolerance, State Feedback, Information Hierarchy). Added severity definitions to relentless-iteration matching ux-audit verbatim. (3) Round 18: Verified extensibility - customization sections are complete and clear. Single-persona usage is documented. (4) Round 19: Fixed confusing 'When NOT to Use' wording in ux-audit (said 'Use for backend-only' when it meant 'Not useful for'). Strengthened value proposition wording. (5) Round 20: Made coverage references conditional in output format templates (N/A option for no-test-runner projects). All output format fields now have corresponding process generation steps. Net change: +5 lines. Pre-existing test failure unchanged: test_skill_paths_resolve expects 28 skills but index has 29.
